name: pr_cep_expjoin_order_alast
drl: rule R when $a : A($ak : k) $b : B(k == $ak, this after[0ms,1000ms] $a) then end
_finding: Temporal join under same-epoch anchor expiry, ANCHOR INSERTED LAST (external review round 21): B then A then advance(2000) - the advance crosses A's expiry (deadline 1000) before the drain, and the join FIRES. Same final (clock, factset) as pr_cep_expjoin_order_blast with the opposite outcome: the insert order of the two events is the sole discriminator - the black-box CEP manifestation of the pinned beta-memory physical-order laws, byte-identical in both engines.
